perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, certain investors have agreed to provide financing (the "Financing") to Pathnet, Inc. (the "Company") subject to the terms of that certain Investment and Stockholders' Agreement, dated October 31, 1997 (the "Investment and Stockholders' Agreement"), by and among the Company, David Schaeffer and the investors identified therein (the "Investors");
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, certain backers have agreed to provide funding (the "Financing") to Pathnet, Inc. (the "Company") subject to the terms of that certain Investment and Stockholders' Agreement, dated October 31, 1997 (the "Investment and Stockholders' Agreement"), by and among the Company, David Schaeffer and the investors identified therein (the "Investors");
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'investors' to 'backers' and 'financing' to 'funding' in the preamble introduces ambiguity. It is uncertain that 'backers' are the same legal entity as 'investors' and 'funding' is the same as 'financing', even if they may be used as synonyms, there is no assurance of that. This contradiction arises from the fact that rights and obligations are later assigned to "Investors" based on the Financing agreement, now there is no clarity if backers are investors and funding is financing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, certain backers have agreed to provide funding (the "Financing") to Pathnet, Inc. (the "Company") subject to the terms of that certain Investment and Stockholders' Agreement, dated October 31, 1997 (the "Investment and Stockholders' Agreement"), by and among the Company, David Schaeffer and the investors identified therein (the "Investors"); WHEREAS, such Financing will significantly benefit the Company and indirectly benefit the above-named Employee, as a stockholder of the Company; and WHEREAS, this Non-Disclosure, Assignment of Inventions and Non-Competition Agreement (this "Agreement") is a condition to the Investment and Stockholders' Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'investors' to 'backers' and 'financing' to 'funding' creates a contradiction because while they can be seen as synonyms, the contract assigns specific rights and obligations to the "Investors" based on the "Financing" agreement. The contradicted text uses "backers" and "funding" in the same section where "investors" are referred to. It introduces ambiguity, making it unclear whether 'backers' are considered the same legal entity as 'investors' and whether 'funding' is equivalent to 'Financing.' This misalignment of terminology, as per the Misaligned Terminology definition, directly contradicts the intended meaning and creates an in-text contradiction, because it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: I understand and agree that my employment creates a relationship of confidence and trust between me and the Company with respect to (a) all proprietary and confidential information of the Company, and (b) the confidential information of others with which the Company has a business relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: I understand and agree that my tenure creates a relationship of confidence and trust between me and the Company with respect to (a) all proprietary and confidential information of the Company, and (b) the confidential information of others with which the Company has a business relationship.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "employment" to "tenure", the agreement creates uncertainty. While generally understood, 'tenure' implies a degree of permanence or job security, which contradicts the 'at will' employment stated in Section 8. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the nature of the employment relationship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Other than the provisions of Section 9 hereof, I understand that this Agreement does not create an obligation on the part of the Company to continue my employment with the Company. I am employed as an employee "at will".
perturbation.justification: The change from 'employment' to 'tenure' creates an in-text contradiction because 'tenure' implies a degree of permanence or job security, which conflicts with the 'at will' employment stated in the Contradicted Text, which explicitly states that the agreement does not guarantee continued employment and that the employee is employed 'at will'. This creates uncertainty regarding the nature of the employment relationship, fulfilling the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: I will not at any time, whether during or after the termination of employment, for any reason whatsoever (other than to promote and advance the business of the Company), reveal to any person or entity (both commercial and non-commercial) or use for any purpose other than the furtherance of the Company's business interests any of the trade secrets or Confidential Information, including, but not limited to, the Company's research and development activities, marketing plans and strategies, pricing and costing policies, customer and supplier lists, and business or financial information of the Company so far as they have come or may come to my knowledge, except as may be required in the ordinary course of performing my duties as an employee of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: I will not at any time, whether during or after the cessation of my role, for any reason whatsoever (other than to promote and advance the business of the Company), reveal to any person or entity (both commercial and non-commercial) or use for any purpose other than the furtherance of the Company's business interests any of the trade secrets or Confidential Information, including, but not limited to, the Company's research and development activities, marketing plans and strategies, pricing and costing policies, customer and supplier lists, and business or financial information of the Company so far as they have come or may come to my knowledge, except as may be required in the ordinary course of performing my duties as a staff member of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'termination of employment' to 'cessation of my role' and 'employee' to 'staff member' introduces ambiguity about the agreement's scope. The term 'role' is much broader than 'employment,' and the latter is not defined. Are consultants included? Also the term 'staff member' is ambigious as it could be any one doing staff for the company. This is not the same as employee, so it creates confusion. Also it can be interpreted in several ways.
perturbation.contradicted_text: While I am employed by the Company and for a period of two (2) years after the termination or cessation of such employment for any reason, I shall not, without the Company's prior written consent, directly or indirectly, alone or as a partner, joint venturer, officer, director, employee, consultant, agent, independent contractor or stockholder of any company or business, engage in any business activity which is or may reasonably be construed to be competitive with the "PathNet Business."
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'termination of employment' with 'cessation of my role' and 'employee' with 'staff member.' The explanation highlights that 'role' is broader than 'employment,' and 'staff member' is more ambiguous than 'employee.' This ambiguity directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which refers to both 'termination or cessation of such employment' and lists 'employee, consultant, agent, independent contractor' distinctly. The change creates uncertainty about whether 'cessation of my role' and 'staff member' includes consultants or independent contractors, thus causing a contradiction as it is unclear who is bound by the non-compete clause.

perturbation.original_text: I hereby assign to the Company the sole and exclusive right to such Inventions. I agree that I will promptly disclose to the Company any and all such Inventions, and that, upon request of the Company, I will execute and deliver any and all documents or instruments and take any other action which the Company shall deem necessary to assign to and vest completely in the Company, to perfect trademark, copyright and patent protection with respect to, or to otherwise protect the Company's trade secrets and proprietary interest in such Inventions.
perturbation.changed_text: I hereby grant to the Company the sole and exclusive license to such Inventions. I agree that I will promptly disclose to the Company any and all such Inventions, and that, upon request of the Company, I will execute and deliver any and all documents or instruments and take any other action which the Company shall deem necessary to license to and vest completely in the Company, to perfect trademark, copyright and patent protection with respect to, or to otherwise protect the Company's trade secrets and proprietary interest in such Inventions.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'assign' to 'grant a license' significantly alters the ownership rights of the inventions. An assignment transfers ownership, while a license only grants permission to use the invention. The text now contradicts itself by requiring actions to 'vest completely in the Company' (assignment) while only licensing the inventions. The original text is clear about assignment and that is what it should be kept to
perturbation.contradicted_text: I expressly understand and agree that any and all right or interest I have or obtain in any designs, trade secrets, technical specifications, technical data, know-how and show-how, internal reports and memoranda, marketing plans, inventions, concepts, ideas, expressions, discoveries, improvements, copyrights, and patent or patent rights conceived, devised, developed, reduced to practice, or which I otherwise have or obtain during the term of this Agreement which relates to the business of the Company or arise out of my employment with the Company are expressly regarded as "works for hire" (the "Inventions"). I hereby grant to the Company the sole and exclusive license to such Inventions. I agree that I will promptly disclose to the Company any and all such Inventions, and that, upon request of the Company, I will execute and deliver any and all documents or instruments and take any other action which the Company shall deem necessary to license to and vest completely in the Company, to perfect trademark, copyright and patent protection with respect to, or to otherwise protect the Company's trade secrets and proprietary interest in such Inventions.
perturbation.justification: The original text assigns ownership of inventions to the company, while the changed text grants only a license. The contradicted text, which includes the changed text, first states the inventions are 'works for hire' (implying company ownership) and then states that the inventor grants the company 'sole and exclusive license'. The change from 'assign' to 'grant a license' in conjunction with the 'works for hire' language introduces a contradiction. A company cannot be granted a license to something they already own by virtue of it being work for hire.

perturbation.original_text: If I am terminated for any reason, in consideration for the Non-competition covenant and the other covenants and agreements set forth in the Agreement, I will receive a salary of $275,000 per annum for one year after such termination payable in bi-weekly installments in accordance with the Company's payroll procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: If my services are terminated for any reason, in consideration for the Non-competition covenant and the other covenants and agreements set forth in the Agreement, I will receive a compensation of $275,000 per annum for one year after such termination payable in bi-weekly installments in accordance with the Company's normal disbursal methods.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'terminated' to 'services are terminated', 'salary' to 'compensation' and 'payroll procedures' to 'normal disbursal methods' creates ambiguity. 'Services' is vague – does it include consultancy? Also 'compensation' is far more broad than salary, and 'normal disbursal methods' implies options other than the standard bi-weekly pay. In the original section, it makes it clear that if terminated for ANY reason, will be given the salaray, the modification undermines that statement
perturbation.contradicted_text: If my services are terminated for any reason, in consideration for the Non-competition covenant and the other covenants and agreements set forth in the Agreement, I will receive a compensation of $275,000 per annum for one year after such termination payable in bi-weekly installments in accordance with the Company's normal disbursal methods.
perturbation.justification: The modification of 'salary' to 'compensation' and 'payroll procedures' to 'normal disbursal methods' introduces ambiguity that directly undermines the original intent of a guaranteed salary paid via standard payroll, especially when the change indicates, 'If my services are terminated for any reason'. The use of 'compensation' in the changed and contradicted text broadens the scope beyond just salary, potentially including benefits or other forms of remuneration, which is not specified. 'Normal disbursal methods' further clouds the issue by suggesting payment options beyond the standard bi-weekly payroll installments, creating uncertainty and contradicting the implicit understanding of a consistent salary payment. The original text is clear that the termination is for any reason, but the modified language and contradicted text could be seen as including or excluding situations that the original text did not.
